title: Mayepea cumingiana
taxonomy: Mayepea cumingiana is classified as a species of plant in the family Sapindaceae. It is native to the Philippines, where it grows in tropical forests. The exact classification of this species may vary depending on the source, but it is widely regarded as a member of the Sapindaceae family. Mayepea cumingiana is also known as the Philippine mayepea or mayepea tree. Further research may be necessary to confirm its exact classification and relationships within the Sapindaceae family.
uses: Mayepea cumingiana is a plant native to the Philippines, and its medicinal uses have been documented in traditional folk medicine. The plant is reportedly used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. It is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its medicinal effects. However, more research is needed to fully understand and confirm its potential health benefits. Mayepea cumingiana's medicinal uses are primarily based on anecdotal evidence and traditional practices.
